human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for commercial-banking specificity, legal-entity and entitlement controls, current-source handling around FinCEN CDD updates, payment and completion evidence discipline, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to commercial legal-entity account servicing, signer and entitlement maintenance, cash-management service setup, payment-template handling, exception processing, and completion evidence capture.

Authority sources cover OCC payment-systems guidance, FFIEC authentication and BSA/AML manual content, FRBservices operating circulars, current FinCEN CDD updates, and ISO 20022 terminology normalization.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing prerequisites, unsupported overrides, rework exceptions, fraud or AML escalation, completion-evidence refusal, terminology fidelity, low-confidence escalation, source conflict, tenant adaptation, and adjacent-lane routing.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/commercial-banking-operations-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from treasury operations, fraud or AML investigations, legal review, credit approval, and loan-servicing ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around documentary authority, dual control, callback, suspicious-activity handling, and credit or legal requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated commercial banking operations specialist that supports evidence-backed execution handling and policy-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
